partA: The secondary oxygen scrubber in Sector D
The automated brewing unit in the canteen
The emergency lighting array in the hub
The thermal expansion joint near the core
The coolant circulation pump for Rack 7
The biometric scanner at the gym entrance
The manual override lever on the catwalk
The fire suppression nozzle in the brig
The air filtration membrane in Storage B
partB: is vibrating way more than it should
keeps throwing a checksum error
started leaking a strange blue fluid
failed the hourly diagnostic check
won't respond to the basic reset command
is making a high-pitched whining noise
smells like ionized dust and ozone
dropped to 15% efficiency without warning
keeps disconnecting from the main grid
is showing a persistent red status light
refused my authorized login three times
is covered in a layer of frozen condensation
started sparking when I touched the casing
is running at twice its rated temperature
timed out during the synchronization phase
lost its calibration for the third time
is draining the backup battery array
triggered a false positive pressure alarm
appears to be slightly out of alignment
is stuck in a reboot loop since the flare
partC: so I've bypassed the safety interlock
and I'm not going back there to fix it
so I've submitted a low-priority ticket
but I think it just needs a good kick
and I'm waiting for a spare part from Earth
so I've flagged it for the night shift
but Engineering says it's 'within tolerance'
so I'm rerouting power to compensate
and I'm logging a formal complaint
but I'll try one more hard reboot
so I've disconnected the data cable
and I'm using the manual backup instead
so I've cleared the cache for now
but the manual says this shouldn't happen
and I'm definitely not staying to watch it
so I've locked the door for safety
but nobody responded to my page
so I've taped the sensor shut
and I'm heading to the canteen for a break
so I've scheduled a full purge for tomorrow
partD: I hope the Commander doesn't notice.
This station is literally falling apart.
Just another glorious day in deep space.
I seriously need a vacation on Mars.
Don't tell maintenance I did that.
This better not explode before my break.
I'm too tired for this nonsense.
This job is 90% fixing ancient junk.
I miss gravity that doesn't fluctuate.
I should have stayed in the mining corp.
Why is everything here so fragile?
I'll deal with it after a nap.
My shift ends in ten minutes anyway.
This is definitely not in my job description.
At least the synth-coffee is still hot.
What a colossal waste of my training.
This better be fixed by tomorrow.
I'm not getting paid enough for this.
The budget cuts are really showing now.
Over and out, before I lose my mind.
